weather.summary: Saturday: 16°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 12°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your little one! From Easter trails and museum adventures to puppet shows and outdoor explorations, there's something to spark joy and creativity for everyone. Let's make it a memorable one!

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, E2 9PA
activities.description: Interactive museum with design, imagine, and play galleries. Features over 2000 toys and a Mini Museum for under 2s with sensory experiences.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a sensory explosion for babies and toddlers, with engaging exhibits and classes like Sound Explorers and Mini Play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station, then walk 5 minutes.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for special toddler-focused sessions.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds and a Story Garden playground. Celebrates children's books with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Celebrates children's books, bringing them to life in an interactive setting. Pop-up exhibits change seasonally, but are always well appointed and colourful.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford station, then walk 5 minutes.
transport.tip: Check the website for specific storytelling and craft sessions.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community farm where children can see and interact with farm animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a chance to see farm animals up close. Great for little ones who love animals.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle, or take a local bus.
transport.tip: Check for feeding times and special events.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small and beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: The wooden playground is safe and fun, great for toddlers to explore.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford station, then walk 10 minutes.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Olympic Park's other attractions.

activities.title: V&A Museum of Childhood (Young V&A)
activities.description: Explore collections of toys, dolls, games and childhood objects from the 1600s to the present day.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of interactive exhibits and displays designed to appeal to young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green, 5 min walk
transport.tip: Check out the daily free activities for children

activities.title: A Squash and a Squeeze
activities.description: Puppet show based on the Julia Donaldson book.
activities.whyItWorks: Short performances built for ages 2-5. The intimate space is good for toddlers.

transport.tip: Check the website for showtimes and booking.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Step into historical homes through the ages. There are lots of hands-on things for them to do, even an old computer with basic games like the original Sims and pinball.
activities.whyItWorks: There are lots of hands-on things for them to do, even an old computer with basic games like the original Sims and pinball.

transport.tip: Pick up an activity sheet or a free explorer backpack from the front desk

activities.title: Free Garden
activities.description: Visit the Barbican Conservatory – free but advance booking is required.
activities.whyItWorks: Wander, look at the beautiful buildings, and find a playground or park to enjoy

transport.tip: Check the website for booking and times.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaur exhibits, interactive displays, and seasonal storytelling sessions. The museum combines learning with fun, offering both educational and festive experiences.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers interactive exhibits suitable for all ages. A great place for kids to discover how the world works.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Visit the Easter Family Festival from April 5-20 for free family-friendly activities.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works through hands-on exhibits. The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for young children to explore construction, water, light, and sound.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers hands-on exhibits, including Wonderlab, which provides experiment demonstrations, talks, activities, and educational play zones.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Book tickets for Wonderlab in advance for a more hands-on experience.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo home to rare species of birds and animals, including wallabies and lemurs.
activities.whyItWorks: Completely free to enter, making it an accessible option for families.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath station, then walk 20 minutes or take a local bus.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath's playgrounds and swimming ponds.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter trail that actually motivates kids to walk. You'll explore the palace grounds looking for gold bunny statues, finishing with a chocolate treat. Works best for: Under-10s who need movement.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court from Liverpool Street
transport.tip: This is an outdoor energy-burner. Dress for wind. Bring snacks. Let them run.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space
activities.whyItWorks: Mini zoo, lots of space to run around

transport.tip: Ideal destination for photography and leisurely strolls

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Most of the action takes place around the enormous Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, but there's also a sensory trail, teepees and plenty of seating for parents to rest their weary legs!
activities.whyItWorks: There are lots of brilliant play spaces in London, so pack a picnic and entertain the kids for free!

transport.tip: A popular park is the Princess of Wales' Memorial Playground

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm
activities.description: Running free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts. With a host of goats, sheep and smaller animals right on your doorstep.
activities.whyItWorks: Located in Zone 1, it is a must for families and animal-lovers

transport.tip: Located in Zone 1, it is a must for families and animal-lovers

activities.title: Paddington the Musical
activities.description: A magical new West End show that's marmalade-packed full of fun!  Join a little bear for a very big adventure! Paddington finds a new home in London in this brand new musical adaptation of the beloved book.
activities.whyItWorks: Opened to glowing reviews and has quickly become a family favourite

transport.tip: Opened to glowing reviews and has quickly become a family favourite

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: Explore a world of play and invention in a recycled playground for children and their grown-ups.
activities.whyItWorks: A play space made out of repurposed materials

transport.tip: Check out the daily free activities for children

activities.title: The Everywhere Bear
activities.description: A puppet show that runs about 45-50 minutes and are built for ages 2-5
activities.whyItWorks: Adaptations of Julia Donaldson's picture books like The Everywhere Bear

transport.tip: The intimate space means your toddler won't feel lost in a sea of strangers, either.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at the Southbank Centre
activities.description: Enjoy family fun with amazing shows, dancing, puppetry and free events this Easter.
activities.whyItWorks: Southbank Centre is a riverside hub of arts, fun and food—perfect for families.

transport.tip: Add in street food, river views and free fun, and you've got a cultural day out everyone enjoys

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: A family Easter adventure with a themed egg trail, indoor and outdoor play, and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Features a giant Easter egg trail, a chance to meet the Easter Bunny, and Easter entertainment with the Hobblers.

transport.tip: Most families spend 4–5 hours, with many staying for the full day to explore everything the park has to offer

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Disney's spectacular musical features stunning puppetry and an unforgettable score by Elton John.
activities.whyItWorks: Easy-going content designed to entertain all ages. During the school holidays, there's even more shows to choose from, as West End theatres accommodate matinee performances of short shows.

transport.tip: If you're looking to get your child into musical theatre, there's no better way.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: An enchanting stage adaptation of the classic children's tale
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of fun for all ages

transport.tip: Check the website for showtimes and booking.

activities.title: The Paddington Bear Experience Easter Events
activities.description: Seasonal character surprises, hidden toy eggs, and that unmistakable Paddington charm.
activities.whyItWorks: The Paddington Bear Experience Easter event runs 28 March to 12 April 2026

transport.tip: Standard tickets start from £52.80 for a family. Book in advance as slots fill up fast!
